XMLATTRIBUTES('duration' AS "name"), GREATEST(ROUND((MA.MAX_LAST_REFRESH_TIME- MA.MAX_SQL_EXEC_START) * 86400), LEAST(MA. SUM_ELAPSED_TIME/1000000, 1), CEIL(MA.MAX_QUEUING_TIME/1000000))), DECODE(MA.SUM_ELAPSED_TIME, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('elapsed_time' AS "name"), MA.SUM_ELAPSED_TIME)), DECODE(MA.MAX_QUEUING_TIME, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('queuing_time' AS "name"), MA.MAX_QUEUING_TIME)), DECODE(MA.SUM_CPU_TIME, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('cpu_time' AS "name"), MA.SUM_CPU_TIME)), DECODE(MA.SUM_USER_IO_WAIT_TIME, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('user_io_wait_time' AS "name"), MA.SUM_USER_IO_WAIT_TIME)), DECODE(MA.SUM_APPLICATION_WAIT_TIME, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('application_wait_time' AS "name"), MA.SUM_APPLICATION_WAIT_TIME)), DECODE(MA.SUM_CONCURRENCY_WAIT_TIME, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('concurrency_wait_time' AS "name"), MA.SUM_CONCURRENCY_WAIT_ TIME)), DECODE(MA.SUM_CLUSTER_WAIT_TIME, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('cluster_wait_time' AS "name"), MA.SUM_CLUSTER_WAIT_TIME)), DECODE(MA.SUM_PLSQL_EXEC_TIME, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('plsql_exec_time' AS "name"), MA.SUM_PLSQL_EXEC_TIME)), DECODE(MA.SUM_JAVA_EXEC_TIME, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('java_exec_time' AS "name"), MA.SUM_JAVA_EXEC_TIME)), DECODE(MA.SUM_OTHER_WAIT_TIME, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('other_wait_time' AS "name"), MA.SUM_OTHER_WAIT_TIME)), DECODE(MA.SUM_FETCHES, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('user_fetch_count' AS "name"), MA.SUM_FETCHES)), DECODE(MA.SUM_BUFFER_GETS, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('buffer_gets' AS "name"), MA.SUM_BUFFER_GETS)), DECODE(MA.SUM_READ_REQS, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUT ES('read_reqs' AS "name"), MA.SUM_READ_REQS)), DECODE(MA.SUM_READ_BYTES, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('read_bytes' AS "name"), MA.SUM_READ_BYTES)), DECODE(MA.SUM_WRITE_REQS, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('write_reqs' AS "name"), MA.SUM_WRITE_REQS)), DECODE(MA.SUM_WRITE_BYTES, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('write_bytes' AS "name"), MA.SUM_WRITE_BYTES)), DECODE(MA.SUM_IO_UNC_BYTES, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('unc_bytes' AS "name"), MA.SUM_IO_UNC_BYTES)), DECODE(MA.SUM_IO_ELIG_BYTES, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('elig_bytes' AS "name"), MA.SUM_IO_ELIG_BYTES)), DECODE(MA.SUM_IO_RET_BYTES, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('ret_bytes' AS "name"), MA.SUM_IO_RET_BYTES)), CASE WHEN MA.SUM_IO_INTER_BYTES IS NULL OR MA.SUM_IO_INTER_BYTES = MA.SUM_IO_BYT ES OR MA.SUM_IO_BYTES = 0 OR NVL(MA.SUM_IO_ELIG_BYTES, 0) = 0 THEN NULL ELSE XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('cell_offload_efficiency' AS "name"), ROUND(MA.SUM_IO_BYTES / DECODE(MA.SUM_IO_INTER_BYTES, 0, 1, MA.SUM_IO_INTER_BYTES), 2)) END, CASE WHEN NVL(MA.SUM_IO_ELIG_BYTES, 0) = 0 OR MA.SUM_IO_BYTES = 0 OR MA.SUM_IO_UNC_BYTES = MA.SUM_IO_RET_BYTES THEN NULL ELSE XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('cell_offload_efficiency2' AS "name"), GREATEST(0, ROUND(100 * (GREATEST(MA.SUM_IO_UNC_BYTES, MA.SUM_IO_ELIG_BYTES) - MA.SUM_IO_RET_BYTES) / MA.SUM_IO_ELIG_BYTES, 2))) END) ) END) FROM MONITOR_AGG MA) ELSE NULL END) FROM (SELECT CASE WHEN V1.XPLAN_XML IS NULL OR V1.XPLAN_XML.EXISTSNODE('/error') > 0 THEN NULL ELSE V1.XPLAN_XML END XPLAN_XML FROM (SELECT CASE WHEN :B36 = 1 THEN DBMS_XPLAN.BUILD_PLAN_XML( TABLE_NAME=>'gv$all_sql_plan', PLAN_TAG=>'plan', FILTER_PREDS=>:B35 , FORMAT=>'-PROJECTION +ALIAS +ADAPTIVE') ELSE NULL END XPLAN_XML FROM DUAL) V1) CONST_VIEW
1bzcdkt9k659yWITH cpu_info AS ( SELECT inst_id, CASE WHEN (cpu_count IS NULL OR cpu_count = 0 OR cpu_count >= num_cpus) THEN num_cpus ELSE cpu_count END cpu_count, process_limit, session_limit FROM TABLE ( gv$(CURSOR( SELECT to_number(userenv('INSTANCE')) AS inst_id, o.num_cpus num_cpus, p.cpu_count cpu_count, p.process_limit process_limit, p.session_limit session_limit FROM (SELECT SUM(value) num_cpus FROM v$osstat WHERE stat_name = 'NUM_CPUS' ) o, (SELECT SUM(case when name = 'cpu_count' then to_number(value) else 0 end) cpu_count, SUM(case wh en name = 'processes' then nvl(to_number(value), 0) else 0 end) process_limit, SUM(case when name = 'sessions' then nvl(to_number(value), 0) else 0 end) session_limit FROM v$system_parameter WHERE name IN ('cpu_count', 'processes', 'sessions') AND con_id = 0 ) p )) ) ), inst_list AS ( SELECT instance_number, instance_name, host_name FROM TABLE ( gv$(CURSOR( SELECT instance_number, instance_name, host_name FROM v$instance )) ) ), db_inst_cpu_cap AS ( SELECT i.instance_number, i.instance_name, i.host_name, c.cpu_count, c.process_limit, c.session_limit FROM cpu_info c, inst_list i WHERE i.instance_number = c.inst_id ), utc_stats AS ( SELECT inst_id , begin_time , end_time , metric_name , value FROM TABLE(GV$(CURSOR ( SELECT TO_NUMBER(USERENV('INSTANCE')) AS inst_id , s.begin_time-times.time_zone AS begin_time, s.end_time -times.time_zone AS end_time, DECODE( s.metric_name , 'Executions Per Sec', 'executions', 'User Calls Per Sec', 'userCalls', 'Total Parse Count Per Sec', 'totalParseCount', 'User Commits Per Sec', 'userCommits', 'User Rollbacks Per Sec', 'userRollbacks', 'Session Count', 'sessionCount', 'Current Logons Count', 'currentLogons', 'Physical Read Total Bytes Per Sec', 'totalphysicalreads', 'Physical Write Total Bytes Per Sec', 'totalphysicalwrites', 'Physical Read Total IO Requests Per Sec', 'phyreadtotalioreqs', 'Physical Write Total IO Requests Per Sec', 'phywritetotalioreqs', 'Hard Parse Count Per Sec', 'hardparses', 'DB Block Changes Per Sec', 'dbblockchanges', 'Logical Reads Per Sec', 'logicalreads', 'Physical Reads Per Sec', 'physicalreads', 'Physical Reads Direct Per Sec', 'phyreadsdirect', 'Redo Writes Per Sec', 'redowrites', 'Redo Generated Per Sec', 'redosize', 'Average Active Sessions', 'avgActiveSessions', 'Background CPU Usage Per Sec', 'avgBackgroundCPUSessions', 'CPU Usage Per Sec', 'avgCPUSessions', 'Process Limit %', 'processLimitPct', 'Session Limit %', 'sessionLimitPct', 'User Limit %', 'userLimitPct', 'Active Parallel Sessions', 'activeParallelSessions', 'Active Serial Sessions', 'activeSerialSessions', 'SQL Service Response Time', 'sqlSvcRespTime') AS metric_name, CASE WHEN metric_name IN ('CPU Usage Per Sec', 'Background CPU Usage Per Sec') THEN (s.value * INTSIZE_CSEC/10000) WHEN metric_name NOT IN ('Session Count', 'Current Logons Count', 'Process Limit %', 'Session Limit %', 'User Limit %', 'Active Parallel Sessions', 'Active Serial Sessions', 'SQL Service Response Time ') THEN (s.value * INTSIZE_CSEC/100) ELSE s.value END AS value FROM v$sysmetric_history s, (SELECT ROUND((CAST(MAX(END_TIME) AS DATE) - CAST(sys_extract_utc(systimestamp) AS DATE)) * 96, 0)/96 AS time_zone, TRUNC ( GREATEST( NVL(CAST(TO_TIMESTAMP_TZ( null, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS, FF3 TZR' ) AS DATE), CAST(sys_extract_utc(systimestamp) AS DATE) - (360 / 86400) ) , CAST(sys_extract_utc(systimestamp) AS DATE) - (1/24) ) , 'MI' ) AS min_time FROM v$sysmetric ) times WHERE s.con_id = 0 AND s.end_time-times.time_zone >= times.min_time AND s.group_id = 2 AND s.metric_name IN ('Executions Per Sec', 'User Calls Per Sec', 'Total Parse Count Per Sec', 'User Commits Per Sec', 'User Rollbacks Per Sec', 'Session Count', 'Current Logons Count', 'Average Active Sessions', 'CPU Usage Per Sec', 'Background CPU Usage Per Sec', 'Physical Read Total Bytes Per Sec', 'Ph ysical Write Total Bytes Per Sec', 'Physical Read Total IO Requests Per Sec', 'Physical Write Total IO Requests Per Sec', 'Hard Parse Count Per Sec', 'DB Block Changes Per Sec', 'Logical Reads Per Sec', 'Physical Reads Per Sec', 'Physical Reads Direct Per Sec', 'Redo Writes Per Sec', 'Redo Generated Per Sec', 'Process Limit %', 'Session Limit %', 'User Limit %', 'Active Parallel Sessions', 'Active Serial Sessions', 'SQL Service Response Time') ) )) ), time_filter AS ( SELECT /*+ NO_MERGE */ TRUNC (GREATEST( NVL(CAST(TO_TIMESTAMP_TZ( null, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS, FF3 TZR' ) AS DATE), CAST(sys_extract_utc(systimestamp) AS DATE) - (360 / 86400)), CAST( sys_extract_utc(systimestamp) AS DATE) - (1/24) ) , 'MI') AS min_time FROM dual ), time_range AS ( SELECT /*+ NO_MERGE */ MIN(et) AS et , MAX(bt) AS bt FROM ( SELECT inst_id , MIN(begin_time) AS bt , MAX(end_time) AS et FROM utc_stats GROUP BY inst_id ) ), buckets AS ( SELECT /*+ NO_MERGE */ begin_time , end_time FROM ( SELECT TRUNC(min_time + (((level-1)/1440) + (1/8640)) , 'MI') AS begin_time , TRUNC(min_time + (((level)/1440) + (1/8640)) , 'MI') AS end_time FROM time_filter CONNECT BY level <= 61 ) WHERE end_time <= ( SELECT et FROM time_range ) AND begin_time >= ( SELECT bt FROM time_range ) ), joined_stats AS ( SELECT s.inst_id , s.metric_name , s.value , b.end_time AS end_time , s.end_time - s.begin_time AS stat_int , least(s.end_time, b.end_time) - greatest(s.begin_time, b.begin_time) AS intersect_int FROM utc_stats s, buckets b WHERE s.end_time >= b.begin_tim e AND s.begin_time <= b.end_time ), row_stats AS ( SELECT inst_id, metric_name , end_time , DECODE(metric_name, 'currentLogons', MAX(value), 'sessionCount', MAX(value), 'processLimitPct', MAX(value), 'sessionLimitPct', MAX(value), 'userLimitPct', MAX(value), 'activeParallelSessions', MAX(value), 'activeSerialSessions', MAX(value), 'sqlSvcRespTime', AVG(value), SUM(value * intersect_int / stat_int)) AS value FROM joined_stats GROUP BY inst_id, metric_name , end_time ), pivoted_stats AS ( SELECT * FROM row_stats PIVOT(SUM(value) FOR metric_name IN ( 'executions' AS executions, 'userCalls' AS userCalls, 'totalParseCount' AS totalParseCount, 'userCommits' AS userCommits, 'userRollbacks' AS userRollbacks, 'sessionCount' AS sessionCount, 'currentLogons' AS currentLogons, 'avgActiveSessions' AS avgActiveSessions, 'avgCPUS essions' AS avgCPUSessions, 'avgBackgroundCPUSessions' AS avgBackgroundCPUSessions, 'totalphysicalreads' AS totalphysicalreads, 'totalphysicalwrites' AS totalphysicalwrites, 'phyreadtotalioreqs' AS phyreadtotalioreqs, 'phywritetotalioreqs' AS phywritetotalioreqs, 'hardparses' AS hardparses, 'redosize' AS redosize, 'redowrites' AS redowrites, 'dbblockchanges' AS dbblockchanges, 'phyreads' AS phyreads, 'phyreadsdirect' AS phyreadsdirect, 'logicalreads' AS logicalreads, 'processLimitPct' AS processLimitPct, 'sessionLimitPct' AS sessionLimitPct, 'userLimitPct' AS userLimitPct, 'activeParallelSessions' AS activeParallelSessions, 'activeSerialSessions' AS activeSerialSessions, 'sqlSvcRespTime' AS sqlSvcRespTime ) ) ), dbload_hist as ( SELECT TO_CHAR(s.end_time, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS') || ' UTC' as sampletime, c.instance_number as insstance_number, c.instance_name as instance_na me, c.host_name as host_name, ( ROUND(NVL(s.userCommits, 0)) + ROUND(NVL(s.userRollbacks, 0)) ) as transactionss, ROUND(NVL(s.executions, 0)) as executions, ROUND(NVL(s.userCalls, 0)) as userCalls, GREATEST(ROUND(NVL(s.totalParseCount, 0)), ROUND(NVL(s.hardparses, 0))) as parses, ROUND(NVL(s.currentLogons, 0)) currentlogons, ROUND(ROUND(NVL(s.dbblockchanges, 0))/60, 2) dbblockchanges_ps, (CASE WHEN ROUND(NVL(c.cpu_count, 0)) > 0 THEN ( ( ROUND(NVL(s.avgBackgroundCPUSessions, 0)/60, 5) + ROUND(NVL(s.avgCPUSessions, 0)/60, 5) ) / c.cpu_count ) * 100 ELSE 0 END) as cpuUtilization, (CASE WHEN ROUND(NVL(c.cpu_count, 0)) > 0 THEN ( ( ROUND(NVL(s.avgBackgroundCPUSessions, 0)/60, 5) ) / c.cpu_count ) * 100 ELSE 0 END) as bgCpuUtilization FROM pivoted_stats s, db_inst_cpu_cap c WHERE s.inst_id = c.instance_number ) SELECT '{"timestamp":"'||sampletime||' ", "instanceNumber":'||insstance_number||', "instanceName":"'||instance_name ||'", "hostName":"'||host_name||'", "TransactionCount":'||transactionss||', "ExecuteCount":'||executions ||', "UserCalls":'||userCalls||', "ParseCount":'||parses||', "CurrentLogons":'||currentlogons ||', "BlockChanges":'||TO_CHAR(dbblockchanges_ps, 'FM99999990.00000')||', "CpuUtilization":' ||TO_CHAR(CASE WHEN cpuUtilization > 100 THEN (CASE WHEN bgCpuUtilization > 100 THEN 0 ELSE bgCpuUtilization END) ELSE cpuUtilization END, 'FM99999990.00000')||'}' FROM dbload_hist WHERE instance_name = (select sys_context('userenv', 'instance_name') from dual) AND ROWNUM <= 5
1kb56hd6c73bvselect count(*) num_windows, sum(case when jobs_started<>jobs_completed then 1 else 0 end) num_jobs_incomplete from (select x.cname_ketcl client_name, sum(case when operation = 'RUN' then 1 else 0 end) as jobs_started, sum(case when operation = 'RUN' and status = 'SUCCEEDED' then 1 else 0 end) as jobs_completed from x$ketcl x, dba_scheduler_job_log jl, (select window_name, window_start_time, window_end_time from ( select window_name, lag(window_name, 1) over (order by log_date ) prev_window_name, operation, lag(operation, 1) over (order by log_date ) prev_operation, lag(log_date, 1) over (order by log_date ) window_start_time, log_date window_end_time from dba_scheduler_window_log where operation in ('OPEN', 'CLOSE')) where operation = 'CLOSE' and prev_operation = 'OPEN' and window_name = prev_window_name) WLOG where (bitand(x.attr_ketcl, 2048) = 0 or 999999 < (select to_number(value) from v$system_parameter where name = '_automatic_maintenance_test')) and x.cid_ketcl > 0 and jl.job_class in (x.hjc_ketcl, x.ujc_ketcl, x.mjc_ketcl) and jl.log_date between wlog.window_start_time and wlog.window_end_time group by x.cname_ketcl, wlog.window_name, wlog.window_start_time, wlog.window_end_time - wlog.window_start_time, wlog.window_end_time ) where client_name = 'auto optimizer stats collection'
1zd5vxxsn83nzSELECT status, status_details FROM wrr$_replays WHERE id = :rep_id
2g73r908mycbgBEGIN dbms_workload_replay_i.group_workload;END;
2sxqgx5hx76qrselect /*+ rule */ bucket, endpoint, col#, epvalue, epvalue_raw, ep_repeat_count, endpoint_enc from histgrm$ where obj#=:1 and intcol#=:2 and row#=:3 order by bucket
3s9qj291717dbSELECT '{' || '"name":"' || a.name || '", ' || '"tablespace":"' || b.tablespace_name || '", ' || '"content":"' || d.contents || '", ' || '"current_size":"' || (b.tablespace_size*d.block_size)/(1024*1024*1024) || '", ' || '"used_space":"'|| (b.used_space*d.block_size)/(1024*1024*1024) || '", ' || '"max_size":"' || c.max_size/(1024*1024*1024) || '", ' || '"pct":"' || 100*(b.used_space*d.block_size/c.max_size) || '", ' || '"type":"' || (CASE WHEN a.name IN (SELECT name FROM v$pdbs) THEN 'pdb' ELSE 'cdb' END) || '"}' AS JSON_OUTPUT FROM v$containers a, cdb_tablespace_usage_metrics b, (SELECT con_id, tablespace_name, SUM(DECODE(maxbytes, 0, bytes, maxbytes)) max_size FROM cdb_data_files GROUP BY con_id, tablespace_name) c, cdb_tablespaces d WHERE a.con_id = b.con_id AND b.con_id = c.con_id AND b.con_id = d.con_id AND b.tablespace_name = c.tablespace_name AND b.tablespace_name = d.tablespace_name AND d.contents != 'UNDO' ORDER BY a.name, b.tablespace_name
4dyfu3pyz39d2BEGIN :ret_val := dbms_workload_replay_i.process_replay_graph( :num_slaves, :flags, :rep_wl_dbid, :rep_wl_id); END;
5dqz0hqtp9fruselect /*+ connect_by_filtering index(sysauth$ i_sysauth1) */ privilege#, bitand(nvl(option$, 0), 72), grantee#, level from sysauth$ connect by grantee#=prior privilege# and privilege#>0 start with grantee#=:1 and privilege#>0
64b91g4br5g6bSELECT disk_number, group_number, write_time FROM V$ASM_DISK_IOSTAT order by disk_number
6hnhqahphpk8nselect free_mb from v$asm_diskgroup_stat where name=:1
7dh638p7rn37kSELECT '{"StorageAllocated":'||TO_CHAR(storagespaceallocated, 'FM99999990.00000')||', "StorageUsed":'||TO_CHAR(storagespaceused, 'FM99999990.00000') ||', "StorageUtilization":'||TO_CHAR(round(storagespaceused/storagespaceallocated * 100, 2), 'FM99999990.00000') ||', "timestamp":"'||TO_CHAR(trunc(sys_extract_utc(systimestamp), 'MI'), 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS') || ' UTC'||'"}' FROM (SELECT round(SUM(allocated_space / 1024), 9) AS storagespaceallocated, round(SUM(used_space / 1024), 9) AS storagespaceused FROM ( SELECT d.tablespace_name, nvl(a.bytes / 1024 / 1024, 0) allocated_space, decode(d.contents, 'UNDO', nvl(u.bytes, 0) / 1024 / 1024, f.used_space *(d.block_size) /(1 024 * 1024)) used_space FROM cdb_tablespaces d, ( SELECT tablespace_name, SUM(bytes) bytes FROM cdb_data_files GROUP BY tablespace_name ) a, ( SELECT tablespace_name, used_space FROM cdb_tablespace_usage_metrics ) f, ( SELECT tablespace_name, SUM(bytes) bytes FROM cdb_undo_extents WHERE status IN ( 'ACTIVE', 'UNEXPIRED' ) GROUP BY tablespace_name ) u WHERE d.tablespace_name = a.tablespace_name (+) AND d.tablespace_name = f.tablespace_name (+) AND d.tablespace_name = u.tablespace_name (+) AND NOT ( d.extent_management = 'LOCAL' AND d.contents = 'TEMPORARY' ) UNION ALL SELECT d.tablespace_name, nvl(a.bytes / 1024 / 1024, 0) allocated_space, nvl(t.bytes, 0) / 1024 / 1024 used_space FROM cdb_tablespaces d, ( SELECT tablespace_name, SUM(bytes) bytes, COUNT(file_id) count FROM cdb_temp_files GROUP BY tablespace_name ) a, ( SELECT ss.tablespace_name, SUM((ss.used_blocks * ts.block_size)) bytes FROM gv$sort_segment ss, cdb_tablespaces ts WHERE ss.tablespace_name = ts.tablespace_name GROUP BY ss.tablespace_name ) t WHERE d.tablespace_name = a.tablespace_name (+) AND d.tablespace_name = t.tablespace_name (+) AND d.extent_management LIKE 'LOCAL' AND d.contents LIKE 'TEMPORARY' UNION ALL ( SELECT NULL, SUM(a.block_size * a.file_size_blks) / 1024 / 1024 allocated_space, SUM(u.records_total * u.recor d_size) / 1024 / 1024 used_space FROM gv$controlfile a, gv$controlfile_record_section u GROUP BY NULL ) UNION ALL ( SELECT NULL, SUM(space_limit) / 1024 / 1024 allocated_space, SUM(space_used) / 1024 / 1024 used_space FROM v$recovery_file_dest ) UNION ALL ( SELECT NULL, SUM(bytes) / 1024 / 1024 allocated_space, SUM(bytes) / 1024 / 1024 used_space FROM gv$log ) ))
865qwpcdyggkkselect spare6 from user$ where user#=:1
881apz9b5j1q2begin :1 := dbms_workload_replay.client_connect( :2, :3, :4, :5, :6 ); end;
9cbh3k57an2a8 select stat_id, sum(value) from gv$database_replay_progress where stat_id in (0, 3, 4, 6, 7) group by stat_id
9jfxqs7ys30px select seq_name, seq_bnm, seq_bow, first_value, last_value, change, num_values, call_ctr, rank from WRR$_REPLAY_SEQ_DATA where file_id = :file_id and call_ctr > :call_ctr order by call_ctr, rank, seq_name, seq_bnm, seq_bow
9zg9qd9bm4spuupdate user$ set spare6=DECODE(to_char(:2, 'YYYY-MM-DD'), '0000-00-00', to_date(NULL), :2) where user#=:1
a0rhzvrg6f1ytUPDATE WRR$_REPLAYS SET NUM_ADMINS = NVL(NUM_ADMINS, 0) + 1 WHERE STATUS = :B1
bmp18qxrgqnd7select a.name, (a.space_limit)/(1024*1024)SPACE_LIMIT, b.file_type, ((b.PERCENT_SPACE_USED/100)*a.SPACE_LIMIT)/(1024*1024) TOTAL_SPACE_USED, b.PERCENT_SPACE_USED TOTAL_PERCENT_SPACE_USED, ((b.PERCENT_SPACE_RECLAIMABLE/100)*a.SPACE_LIMIT)/(1024*1024)space_reclaimable, b.PERCENT_SPACE_RECLAIMABLE, (((b.PERCENT_SPACE_USED-b.PERCENT_SPACE_RECLAIMABLE)/100)*a.SPACE_LIMIT)/(1024*1024), b.PERCENT_SPACE_USED-b.PERCENT_SPACE_RECLAIMABLE PERCENT_SPACE_USED from V$RECOVERY_FILE_DEST a, V$FLASH_RECOVERY_AREA_USAGE b order by b.file_type
c3zymn7x3k6wyselect obj#, dataobj#, part#, hiboundlen, hiboundval, flags, ts#, file#, block#, pctfree$, initrans, maxtrans, analyzetime, samplesize, rowcnt, blevel, leafcnt, distkey, lblkkey, dblkkey, clufac, pctthres$, length(bhiboundval), bhiboundval from indpart$ where bo# = :1 order by part#
d8dama8rwdgucSELECT /*+ NO_STATEMENT_QUEUING RESULT_CACHE (SYSOBJ=TRUE SHELFLIFE=30) */ "TABLESPACE_NAME", "BYTES", "MAXBYTES" FROM "SYS"."DBA_DATA_FILES" "K" WHERE 1=1
dp8ndtnqfpy7rselect name, value from v$dataguard_stats where value is not null union all select 'database_role' as name, decode (dest_role, 'PRIMARY DATABASE', 'PRIMARY', dest_role) as value from v$dataguard_config a, v$database b where a.db_unique_name=b.db_unique_name union all select 'database_role' as name, 'NONDG' as value from dual where not exists ( select 'database_role' as name, dest_role as value from v$dataguard_config a, v$database b where a.db_unique_name=b.db_unique_name ) union all select 'remote_archive_dest_count' as name , to_char(count(*)) as value from v$archive_dest where target='REMOTE' or target='STANDBY'
f705bwx3q0ydqselect count(*) from dba_autotask_window_clients c, (select window_name, max(log_date) max_log_date from dba_scheduler_window_log where operation = 'OPEN' group by window_name) wo, (select window_name, max(log_date) max_log_date from dba_scheduler_window_log where operation = 'CLOSE' group by window_name) wc where c.window_name = wo.window_name and c.optimizer_stats = 'ENABLED' and wo.window_name = wc.window_name and wo.max_log_date < wc.max_log_date and wc.window_name not in (select h.window_name from dba_autotask_job_history h where h.client_name = 'auto optimizer stats collection' and h.job_start_time between wo.max_log_date and wc.max_log_date)
f7xnvp44nukp7insert into wrm$_snapshot_details (snap_id, dbid, instance_number, table_id, begin_time, end_time, step_id) values (:snap_id, :dbid, :instance_number, :bind1, :bind2, :bind3, :step_id)
fhf8upax5cxszBEGIN sys.dbms_auto_report_internal.i_save_report (:rep_ref, :snap_id, :pr_class, :rep_id, :suc); END;
fq624v7sdrawhselect count(*) from dba_replay_workloads
ftkdxsra45vppselect status, to_char(cast(start_time as timestamp with time zone), 'yyyy-MM-dd"T"HH24:mi:ss.FF3TZH:TZM'), to_char(cast(end_time as timestamp with time zone), 'yyyy-MM-dd"T"HH24:mi:ss.FF3TZH:TZM'), object_type, operation from v$rman_status where recid = (select max(recid) from v$rman_status where operation = 'BACKUP' and object_type like 'DB%') or recid = (select max(recid) from v$rman_status where operation = 'BACKUP' and object_type = 'ARCHIVELOG')

Active Session History(ASH) Report

  • Top SQL with Top Events
  • Top SQL with Top Row Sources
  • Top Sessions
  • Top Blocking Sessions
  • Top PL/SQL Procedures
  • Top Events
  • Top Event P1/P2/P3 Values
  • Top DB Objects
  • Activity Over Time
Back to Top

Top SQL with Top Events

  • Top SQL statements by DB Time along with the top events by DB Time for those SQLs.
  • % Activity is the percentage of DB Time due to the SQL.
  • % Event is the percentage of DB Time due to the event that the SQL is waiting on.
  • % Row Source is the percentage of DB Time due to the row source for the SQL waiting on the event.
  • Executions is the number of executions of the SQL that were sampled in ASH.
SQL IDPlan HashExecutions% ActivityEvent% EventTop Row Source% Row SourceSQL TextContainer Name
ca5cs5n191fd14188824246481.57 CPU + Wait for CPU1.57SELECT STATEMENT1.57 ** SQL Text Not Available **TLRAPATPDB

Back to Active Session History(ASH) Report
Back to Top

Top SQL with Top Row Sources

  • Top SQL statements by DB Time along with the top row sources by DB Time for those SQLs.
  • % Activity is the percentage of DB Time due to the SQL.
  • % Row Source is the percentage of DB Time spent on the row source by that SQL.
  • % Event is the percentage of DB Time spent on the event by the SQL executing the row source.
  • Executions is the number of executions of the SQL that were sampled in ASH.
SQL IDPlan HashExecutions% ActivityRow Source% Row SourceTop Event% EventSQL TextContainer Name
ca5cs5n191fd14188824246481.57 SELECT STATEMENT1.57CPU + Wait for CPU1.57 ** SQL Text Not Available **TLRAPATPDB

Back to Active Session History(ASH) Report
Back to Top

Top Sessions

  • '# Samples Active' shows the number of ASH samples in which the session was found waiting for that particular event. The percentage shown in this column is calculated with respect to wall clock time and not total database activity.
  • 'XIDs' shows the number of distinct transaction IDs sampled in ASH when the session was waiting for that particular event
Sid, Serial#% ActivityEvent% EventUserProgram# Samples ActiveXIDs

Activity Over Time

  • Analysis period is divided into smaller time slots as indicated in the 'Slot Time (Duration)'.
  • Top 3 events are reported in each of those slots
  • 'Slot Count' shows the number of ASH samples in that slot
  • 'Event Count' shows the number of ASH samples waiting for that event in that slot
  • '% Event' is 'Event Count' over all ASH samples in the analysis period

ADDM Task ADDM:348068458_53928

          ADDM Report for Task 'ADDM:348068458_53928'
          -------------------------------------------

Analysis Period
---------------
AWR snapshot range from 53927 to 53928.
Time period starts at 24-NOV-25 11.00.04 AM
Time period ends at 24-NOV-25 12.00.11 PM

Analysis Target
---------------
Database 'TLRAPAT' with DB ID 348068458.
Database version during analysis period was 19.28.0.0.0.
Database version when ADDM was executed was 19.0.0.0.0.
All recommendations generated by ADDM are valid for database version 19.0.0.0.0, the version in which ADDM was executed.
ADDM performed an analysis of all instances.
ADDM detected that the database was open in READ-ONLY mode.
ADDM detected that the database type is MULTITENANT DB.

Activity During the Analysis Period
-----------------------------------
Total database time was 14507 seconds.
The average number of active sessions was 4.02.

Summary of Findings
-------------------
   Description                   Active Sessions      Recommendations
                                 Percent of Activity
   ----------------------------  -------------------  ---------------
1  Unusual "Network" Wait Event  4 | 99.37            7


~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~


          Findings and Recommendations
          ----------------------------

Finding 1: Unusual "Network" Wait Event
Impact is 4 active sessions, 99.37% of total activity.
------------------------------------------------------
Wait event "SQL*Net more data from client" in wait class "Network" was
consuming significant database time.

Instances that were significantly affected by this finding:
    Number  Name      Percent Impact  ADDM Task Name
    ------  --------  --------------  --------------
    2       TLRAPAT2  49.97           ADDM:348068458_2_53928
    3       TLRAPAT3  25.02           ADDM:348068458_3_53928
    4       TLRAPAT4  25.02           ADDM:348068458_4_53928

   Recommendation 1: Application Analysis
   Estimated benefit is 4 active sessions, 99.37% of total activity.
   -----------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Investigate the cause for high "SQL*Net more data from client" waits.
      Refer to Oracle's "Database Reference" for the description of this wait
      event.

   Recommendation 2: Application Analysis
   Estimated benefit is 4 active sessions, 99.37% of total activity.
   -----------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Investigate the cause for high "SQL*Net more data from client" waits in
      Service "TLRAPAT_BDC7.ocac2.pat.tdbank.ca".
   Rationale
      The service connected to the root container.

   Recommendation 3: Application Analysis
   Estimated benefit is 4 active sessions, 99.37% of total activity.
   -----------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Investigate the cause for high "SQL*Net more data from client" waits
      with P1 ("driver id") value "1413697536" and P2 ("#bytes") value "4".

   Recommendation 4: Application Analysis
   Estimated benefit is 1 active sessions, 24.86% of total activity.
   -----------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Investigate the cause for high "SQL*Net more data from client" waits in
      Module "oracle@batlrdbt02 (TNS V1-V3)".
   Rationale
      The module activity was in the root container.

   Recommendation 5: Application Analysis
   Estimated benefit is 1 active sessions, 24.86% of total activity.
   -----------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Investigate the cause for high "SQL*Net more data from client" waits in
      Module "oracle@batlrdbt03 (TNS V1-V3)".
   Rationale
      The module activity was in the root container.

   Recommendation 6: Application Analysis
   Estimated benefit is 1 active sessions, 24.86% of total activity.
   -----------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Investigate the cause for high "SQL*Net more data from client" waits in
      Module "oracle@batlrdbt04 (TNS V1-V3)".
   Rationale
      The module activity was in the root container.

   Recommendation 7: Application Analysis
   Estimated benefit is 1 active sessions, 24.79% of total activity.
   -----------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Investigate the cause for high "SQL*Net more data from client" waits in
      Module "oracle@batlrdbt01 (TNS V1-V3)".
   Rationale
      The module activity was in the root container.

   Symptoms That Led to the Finding:
   ---------------------------------
      Wait class "Network" was consuming significant database time.
      Impact is 4 active sessions, 99.37% of total activity.

~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

Exadata Griddisks

  • Griddisks on the storage servervs
  • Disk Type <F|H|M>/<size>: F-Flash, H-Harddisk, M-XRMEM
  • # CachedBy - # of flash devices caching the griddisk on the cell(s)
  • Size (GB) - Griddisk: indicates size of individual Griddisks in the cells
  • Size (GB) - Cell Total: indicates total size per cell
  • Size (GB) - System Total: indicates total size over all cells

Cache Savings

  • Disk write savings (overwrites) - writes absorbed by flash cache that would have otherwise gone to disk
  • Database Flash Cache Hit% - percentage of database reads from all instances satisfied from Flash Cache
  • Database XRMEM Cache Hit% - percentage of database reads from all instances satisfied from XRMEM Cache
  • Database XRMEM Cache RDMA Hit% - percentage of database reads from all instances satisfied from XRMEM Cache, including RDMA reads
  • Cell Flash Cache Hit% - percentage of cell reads satisfied from Flash Cache
  • Cell XRMEM Cache Hit% - percentage of cell reads satisfied from XRMEM Cache

Single Block Reads

  • cell single block physical read wait time for the database, not restricted to an instance
  • % of small reads from flash/disk from the cells, not restricted to this database or instance
  • small reads for flash/disk from the cells, not restricted to this database or instance

  • small reads include all file types, and is based on the IO request size on the cell
  • small reads histogram on the cell start at <16us
  • Total Small Reads/s - small reads/s for the entire system for the disk type
  • Cell Small Reads/s - average small reads/s for a cell for the disk type
  • Disk Small Reads/s - average small reads/s for a disk for the disk type
  • When % of Total Waits is < 1, the count is shown in parenthesis

ASH Samples for Single Block Reads

  • % of Total Samples - percentage of samples for the cell/wait event over all ASH samples
  • % of Wait Event - percentage of samples for the cell/wait event over the samples for the wait event. This is the distribution of a wait event across the cells
  • % of Cell - percentage of samples for the cell/wait event over the single block read samples for the cell. This is the wait distribution within the cell
  • Total - total across all the single block read wait events
  • # of ASH samples: 3058
  • # of ASH samples for cell single block reads:3 (.1%)

Exadata Outlier Summary

  • Exadata Outlier Summary
    • Cell Level
    • Disk Level

Outlier Summary - Cell Level

  • Outliers are cells whose average performance is outside the normal range, where normal range is -/+ 1 standard deviation
  • Outlier cells must have a mininum of 10 IOPs. Idle cells are not considered for outlier analysis.
  • Outliers for hard disks are displayed when Hard Disk IOPs exceeds 255.6 (1% of maximum capacity of 25,560)
  • Outliers for flash disks are displayed when Flash Disk IOPs exceeds 79384 (1% of maximum capacity of 7,938,400)
  • Outliers for flash disks will not be displayed. There are only 51,309 flash IOPs
  • A 'v' and a dark yellow background indicates an outlier value below the low range
  • A '^' and a light red background indicates an outlier value above the high range
  • A '*' and a dark red background indicates over maximum capacity
  • A 'o' and an orange background indicates high standard deviation (high variability in data set)
  • Disk Type <F|H|M>/<size>: F-Flash, H-Hard Disk, M-XRMEM; XRMEM I/O only include remote I/Os processed by cellsrv
  • Maximum cell capacity for hard disks: IOPS: H/20.0T: 2,556 | IO MB/s: H/20.0T: 1,776
  • Maximum cell capacity for flash disks: IOPS: F/6.2T: 793,840 | IO MB/s: F/6.2T: 45,000
  • There is insufficient activity on Flash Disks, and Flash Disk outliers will not be displayed
  • There are no cell outliers on hard disks

Back to Exadata Outlier Summary
Back to Exadata Resource Statistics

Outlier Summary - Disk Level

  • Outliers are disks whose average performance is outside the normal range, where normal range is -/+ 3 standard deviation
  • Outlier disks must have a mininum of 10 IOPs. Idle disks are not considered for outlier analysis.
  • Outliers for hard disks are displayed when Hard Disk IOPs exceeds 255.6 (1% of maximum capacity of 25,560)
  • Outliers for flash disks are displayed when Flash Disk IOPs exceeds 79384 (1% of maximum capacity of 7,938,400)
  • Outliers for flash disks will not be displayed. There are only 51,309 flash IOPs
  • A 'v' and a dark yellow background indicates an outlier value below the low range
  • A '^' and a light red background indicates an outlier value above the high range
  • A '*' and a dark red background indicates over maximum capacity
  • A 'o' and an orange background indicates high standard deviation (high variability in data set)
  • Disk Type <F|H|M>/<size>: F-Flash, H-Hard Disk, M-XRMEM; XRMEM I/O only include remote I/Os processed by cellsrv
  • Maximum hard disk capacity: IOPS: H/20.0T: 213 | IO MB/s: H/20.0T: 148
  • Maximum flash disk capacity: IOPS: F/6.2T: 198,460 | IO MB/s: F/6.2T: 11,250
  • There is insufficient activity on Flash Disks, and Flash Disk outliers will not be displayed
  • There are no disk outliers on hard disks

Back to Exadata Outlier Summary
Back to Exadata Resource Statistics

Exadata OS IO Statistics - Outlier Cells

  • These statistics are collected by the OS on the cells and are not restricted to this database or instance
  • Outliers are cells whose average performance is outside the normal range, where normal range is -/+ 1 standard deviation
  • Outlier cells must have a mininum of 10 IOPs. Idle cells are not considered for outlier analysis.
  • Outliers for hard disks are displayed when Hard Disk IOPs exceeds 255.6 (1% of maximum capacity of 25,560)
  • Outliers for flash disks are displayed when Flash Disk IOPs exceeds 79384 (1% of maximum capacity of 7,938,400)
  • Outliers for flash disks will not be displayed. There are only 51,309 flash IOPs
  • A 'v' and a dark yellow background indicates an outlier value below the low range
  • A '^' and a light red background indicates an outlier value above the high range
  • A '*' and a dark red background indicates over maximum capacity
  • A 'o' and an orange background indicates high standard deviation (high variability in data set)
  • Disk Type <F|H|M>/<size>: F-Flash, H-Hard Disk, M-XRMEM; XRMEM I/O only include remote I/Os processed by cellsrv
  • %Total - Avg [IOPs |IO MB/s] of the cell as a percentage of total [IOPs | IO MB/s] for the disk type
  • There are no cell outliers on hard disks

Exadata OS IO Latency - Outlier Cells

  • These statistics are collected by the OS on the cells and are not restricted to this database or instance
  • Outliers are cells whose average performance is outside the normal range, where normal range is -/+ 1 standard deviation
  • Outlier cells must have a mininum of 10 IOPs. Idle cells are not considered for outlier analysis.
  • Outliers for hard disks are displayed when Hard Disk IOPs exceeds 255.6 (1% of maximum capacity of 25,560)
  • Outliers for flash disks are displayed when Flash Disk IOPs exceeds 79384 (1% of maximum capacity of 7,938,400)
  • Outliers for flash disks will not be displayed. There are only 51,309 flash IOPs
  • A 'v' and a dark yellow background indicates an outlier value below the low range
  • A '^' and a light red background indicates an outlier value above the high range
  • A 'o' and an orange background indicates high standard deviation (high variability in data set)
  • Disk Type <F|H|M>/<size>: F-Flash, H-Hard Disk, M-XRMEM; XRMEM I/O only include remote I/Os processed by cellsrv
  • There are no cell outliers on hard disks

  • Outlier cells must have a mininum of 10 IOPs. Idle cells are not considered for outlier analysis.

  • Outliers for flash disks are displayed when Flash Disk IOPs exceeds 79384 (1% of maximum capacity of 7,938,400)

  • A 'o' and an orange background indicates high standard deviation (high variability in data set)

Exadata Smart Statistics

Back to Top

Smart IO Statistics

  • Smart IO

Smart IO

  • These statistics are collected by the cells and are not restricted to this database or instance
  • MB Requested - on-disk (logical) size eligible for smart scan
  • Eligible for Smart IO - actual (physical) size eligible for smart scan
  • Storage Index - bytes saved by storage index and percentage of requested bytes saved by storage index
  • Flash Cache - bytes read from flash cache and percentage of requested bytes read from flash cache
  • Hard Disk - bytes read from hard disk
  • Columnar Cache - bytes read from columnar cache
  • Offload - bytes processed by the cells and not returned to the database
  • Passthru - bytes returned as-is to the database (for reasons other than high cell cpu) and percentage of requested bytes returned as-is to the database
  • Reverse Offload - bytes returned as-is to the database due to high cell cpu and percentage of requested bytes returned as-is to the database
  • CC Eligible - bytes eligible for reading from columnar cache
  • CC saved - bytes saved by columnar cache
  • Ordered by Total MB Requested desc
MB Requested Eligible for Smart IO Storage Index Flash Cache Hard Disk Columnar Cache Offload Passthru Reverse Offload CC Eligible CC Saved

Back to Exadata Statistics

Flash Log Statistics

  • Flash Log
  • Redo Write Histogram

Flash Log

  • These statistics are collected by the cells and are not restricted to this database or instance
  • Write Requests Flash Log - writes serviced by flash log
  • Write Requests Redo Log - redo writes serviced including those that bypass flash log
    Database redo log writes are typically written to both flash log and redo log
  • FC Writes - redo writes absorbed by flash cache that would have otherwise gone to database redo logs on disk
  • FC Skips - redo writes bypassing flash cache
  • Write MB - write bytes serviced by flash log
  • First Writes Flash Log - writes written first to smart flash log
  • First Writes DB Log - writes written first to database redo log file
  • % Efficiency - percentage when flash log prevented writes from exceeding the threshold
  • Prevented - flash log writes prevented from exceeding outlier threshold
  • Actual - flash log writes that exceeded outlier threshold
  • Disk I/O Errors - disk I/O errors encountered by flash log (should be 0)
  • Flash I/O Errors - flash I/O errors encountered by flash log (should be 0)
  • Keep - redo saved on flash due to disk I/O errors (should be 0)
  • Skip Count - redo writes not serviced by flash log (should be 0)
  • Ordered by Total Write Requests desc
Write Requests Write MB First Writes Outliers Errors

Redo Write Histogram

  • log file parallel write is for the entire database, not restricted to an instance
  • Redo log write completions are collected by the cells
  • Cells for redo log write completions are ordered by # of writes >= 512ms
  • Redo log write completion Avg Wait is the average time for the redo log write request, including network overhead
  • Redo log write completion Average IO Time is the average time for the IO request
  • When %Total is < 1, the count is shown in parenthesis

Back to Exadata Flash Cache
Back to Exadata Smart Statistics

Flash Cache User Reads - Skips

  • These statistics are collected by the cells and are not restricted to this database or instance
  • Flash Cache User Read Skips are reads that bypass the flash cache
  • Total Skipped includes all reads that have bypassed flash cache
  • Only the following possible reasons for bypassing the flash cache are displayed:
  • Storage Clause - flash cache skipped due to storage clause
  • IOReason - flash cache skipped due to IO reason sent by the database
  • GridDisk Policy - flash cache skipped due to griddisk caching policy
  • Database - flash cache skipped because database has flashcache="off"
  • Large IO - flash cache skipped due to size of IO
  • Throttle IO - flash cache skipped due to throttling
  • Throttle Large IO - flash cache skipped due to exceeding limit for outstanding large IOs
  • Unaligned - flash cache skipped due to unaligned reads

Back to Exadata Flash Cache
Back to Exadata Smart Statistics

Flash Cache User Writes - Skips

  • These statistics are collected by the cells and are not restricted to this database or instance
  • Flash Cache User Writes Skips are writes that bypass the flash cache
  • Total Skipped includes all writes that have bypassed flash cache
  • Only the following possible reasons for bypassing the flash cache are displayed:
  • Storage Clause - flash cache skipped due to storage clause
  • Storage Hint - flash cache skipped due to IO hint passed by database
  • GridDisk Policy - flash cache skipped due to griddisk caching policy
  • Database - flash cache skipped because database has flashcache="off"
  • Large IO - flash cache skipped due to size of IO
  • Throttle IO - flash cache skipped due to throttling
  • Unaligned - flash cache skipped due to unaligned write

Back to Exadata Flash Cache
Back to Exadata Smart Statistics

Flash Cache User Writes - Large Write Rejections

  • These statistics are collected by the cells and are not restricted to this database or instance
  • Large writes may be rejected for the following reasons:
  • Disk Not Busy - IORM determined the hard disk is not busy
  • ASM - tagged as not cacheable by ASM
  • Throttle - large writes rejected due to throttling
  • CG Thrashing - large writes are causing flash cache group thrashing
  • LW Thrashing - large writes are causing thrashing
  • Max Limit - flash cache size for large writes is at flash cache group limit
  • Global Limit - flash cache size for large writes is over global limit
  • Flash Wear - large writes are causing excessive flash wear
  • Flash Busy - flash is busy
  • Keep - keep needs the cache lines
  • Internal - rejections for internal reasons
  • Unknown - rejections for unknown reasons
Eligible Absorbed Rejections Rejections per Second
Cell Name Total per Second Total per Second Total per Second Disk Not Busy ASM Throttle CG Thrashing LW Thrashing Max Limit Global Limit Flash Wear Flash Busy Keep Internal Unknown

Flash Cache Redo Caching

  • These statistics are collected by the cells and are not restricted to this database or instance
  • Flash cache may cache redo writes that would otherwise go to disk
  • This works with, and does not replace, the use of flash log
  • Total Writes - redo writes submitted, does not included population writes
  • FC Writes - redo writes absorbed into flash cache
  • Pop Writes - population writes into flash cache for redo reads

Flash Cache Internal Reads

  • These statistics are collected by the cells and are not restricted to this database or instance
  • Read to Disk Write - reads from flash cache to write to hard disk
  • Disk Writer IO Detail - actual number of IOs
  • Aging Writes - number of writes to allow data to get into flash cache
  • DataSync Writes - number of proactive writes
  • Flush Writes - number of writes due to griddisk flush
  • LW DataSync Writes - number of writes to sync LW to disk
  • Ordered by Total Read Reqs desc

Back to Exadata Flash Cache
Back to Exadata Smart Statistics

Flash Cache Replacements

  • Replacements indicate activity within flash cache
  • OLTP [Succeeded|Failed] - number of allocations requested for OLTP cachelines
  • Scan [Succeeded|Failed] - number of allocations requested for Scan cachelines
  • LW [Succeeded|Failed] - number of allocations requested for LW cachelines
  • Repl Reqs - number of replacements requests
  • Main - number of replacements on MAIN list
  • Zero Hit - number of replacements of cachelines that had zero hits
  • Victim - number of victims searched for allocation requests
  • Avg Search - average search length to find a replacement victim

Flash Cache Internal Writes

  • These statistics are collected by the cells and are not restricted to this database or instance
  • The top cells by Total Write Requests are displayed
  • Population - population writes due to read misses
  • Metadata - Write-Back Flash Cache metadata persistence writes
  • Population Writes for columnar cache - top level call that can result in multiple writes to flash
  • ordered by Total Write requests desc

Flash Columnar Cache Evictions

  • These statistics are collected by the cells and are not restricted to this database or instance
  • Rewrite - eviction to rewrite in another format
  • No Cache - eviction due to IORM plan changes or flush
  • Aged Out - aged out of flash cache
  • Writes - invalidated due to writes
  • Extent Drop - evicted due to extent drop
  • Error - invalidated due to error
  • Misc - evicted by smart scan for other reasons
  • Unknown - eviction for unknown reason

XRMEM Columnar Cache Evictions

  • These statistics are collected by the cells and are not restricted to this database or instance
  • Rewrite - eviction to rewrite in another format
  • No Cache - eviction due to IORM plan changes or flush
  • Aged Out - aged out of XRMEM cache
  • Write - invalidated due to writes
  • Extent Drop - evicted due to extent drop
  • Error - invalidated due to error
  • Misc - evicted by smart scan for other reasons
  • Unknown - eviction for unknown reason

Cancelled IOs - Cells
  • Disk Failure - IOs were stuck and cancelled due to disk failure
  • Reads - cancelled reads due to possible disk failure (hair trigger)
  • Writes - cancelled writes due to possible disk failure (hair trigger)
  • Unsubmitted - IOs were cancelled before reaching the OS
  • %Cancellable - IOs cancelled for the specified reason as a percentage of Cancellable IOs
